No Poverty.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly supports contributions to eradicating poverty.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly supports contributions to ending hunger or improving food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course aims to help students 'understand themselves' and 'improve communication with others,' which reasonably infers a contribution to mental well-being (Target 3.4). Both models converged on this score.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides tertiary education (Target 4.3). It also fosters 'communication ability' and 'practical ability' and explores 'Taiwanese drama forms,' promoting cultural diversity and relevant skills (Target 4.7).

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course involves 'drama production' and explores 'Taiwanese drama forms,' promoting local culture and providing skills for creative jobs, aligning with Target 8.9.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course explicitly lists 'innovation ability' and 'practical ability' as core competencies, applied through 'drama production,' supporting creative industries and innovation (Targets 9.2, 9.5).
